Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(5 comments)

Thanks for the quick patch, but it's hard to know whether this will actually fix the problem without running repeated tests, since it is only failing about 30% of the time.

If it isn't clear why sanity-lnet.sh is not being marked FAIL when a subtest itself is failing, at a minimum this patch can fix the problem and the test results need to be manually inspected for success.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

First revision of this patch was a "fortestonly testlist=sanity-lnet" with the test script modified to run a lot more iterations of the test_230 portion that failed. I did make a mistake to ask to for too high conns_per_peer which caused hitting the limit of available connections (we can't go higher that 1024), but I didn't see random failures at lower conns_per_peer. I'll redo the test revision with appropriate conns_per_peer boundaries to make it clear.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Looks good to me.

Since this patch is fixing the test_230 failure, it doesn't need to be rebased on top of https://review.whamcloud.com/45342, rather the reverse is true.  It should first verify that the test_230 failure is now causing the patch to fail, then be rebased on top of this one.

commit.subject: LU-15089 tests: allow enough time to create tcp connections
commit.message:
LU-15089 tests: allow enough time to create tcp connections

Allow enough time to create tcp connections before counting them
when testing socklnd conns_per_peer setting in sanity-lnet test_230

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
